name: Bhai Gurpreet Singh (Bathinda)
body: The jatha of Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji has gained popularity in recent years and deservedly so. Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji hails from Bathinda but was born in his maternal grandparents’ city of Ludhiana to Mata Harvinder Kaur Ji and Sardar Kanwaljit Singh Ji on 12 th August 1980. Bhai Sahib Ji attended school at Guru Nanak Dev Senior Secondary School in Bathinda. Alongside his formal education, he also started taking music and religious lessons from his grandfather, Sardar Achar Singh Ji from the age of six to seven years old. His keen interest in music led him to learn from various teachers over the years. Sardar Achar Singh Ji played a major role in his development and even during difficult times, he would travel quite a distance to Ragi Lal Singh Ji so that his grandson was able to learn Kirtan. He also took tabla lessons for five years from Dr. Jagmohan Sharma.

Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji went on to study at DAV College in Bathinda and subsequently obtained a Sangeet Visharad Diploma for vocals and instrumental from Pracheen Kala Kendra in Chandigarh. While in university, he was awarded three gold and two silver medals for his tabla performances. He has also learned a lot from Bhai Kuldeep Singh Ji, who is currently serving as Hazoori Raagi in Sri Harmandir Sahib, Amritsar.

Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji is greatly influenced by the Kirtan of Bhai Niranjan Singh Ji and Bhai Sarabjit Singh Ji Laddi as well. He has had the opportunity to meet and perform together with Bhai Sarabjit Singh Laddi many times and he fondly recounts the experience as being an opportunity to learn a lot. Besides his passion for Kirtan, Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji also loves to listen to instrumental music such as the flute and sitar.

Bhai Sahib Ji has traveled extensively over the years. He has been to the United States six times and to Europe almost ten times. Some of the European countries he has visited include Germany, France, Italy, Denmark and Norway. He has also been to Canada, Australia and the United Kingdom. Currently, Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji has been serving as a Hazoori Raagi in Takhat Sri Damdama Sahib Ji for slightly over one year. He is accompanied in his ‘jatha’ by his brother, Charanjit Singh on tabla and Jaskaran Singh on the side harmonium.

He has also released several albums which are available in the market and have been very popular among listeners. His albums include ‘Ek Naam Bovho’, ‘Gusayi Partap Tuharo Deetha’, ‘Tu Prabh Daata’ and several others. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Gupreet Singh Ji to share, please contact us.
